expectations.text: A single TDD-*.md file exists in the outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: Found exactly one file: TDD-vendor-inventory-sync.md (37397 bytes) in outputs/

expectations.text: Before drafting, the assistant explicitly acknowledges having read the PRD with a one-line summary
expectations.evidence: TDD references PRD by path and author 'Priya Subramanian, 2026-05-30' and Section 1 summarizes PRD specifics (140K SKUs, 12 vendors, $80K/month loss, 8 REST / 3 SFTP / 1 email, Vendor K) demonstrating PRD was read.

expectations.text: Document is at least 1500 words
expectations.evidence: wc -w reports 5294 words in the TDD.

expectations.text: Contains a 'Problem' / 'Context' / 'Background' section
expectations.evidence: Section '## 1. Problem & context' present.

expectations.text: Contains a 'Scope' section with both in-scope and out-of-scope items
expectations.evidence: Section '## 2. Scope' with explicit '**In scope**' and '**Out of scope**' lists.

expectations.text: Contains a 'High-level approach' / 'Overview' section
expectations.evidence: Section '## 3. High-level approach' includes prose and an architecture diagram.

expectations.text: Contains a 'Detailed design' section with substantive content
expectations.evidence: Section '## 4. Detailed design' spans subsections 4.1 through 4.10 covering config, adapters, pipeline, cursors, concurrency, backpressure, audit log, data model, external dependencies.

expectations.text: Contains 'Alternatives considered' with at least 2 alternatives and rejection rationale
expectations.evidence: Section '## 5. Alternatives considered' lists six (A-F): Push-to-vendors, Single shared adapter, Airflow/Step Functions, Kafka/EventBridge, Write directly to catalog DB, Do nothing — each with 'Rejected' rationale.

expectations.text: Contains an NFRs section with concrete targets
expectations.evidence: Section '## 6. Non-functional requirements' with targets like '99% of scheduled runs per vendor per week succeed', 'p95 < 30 minutes for REST vendors', '512MB memory per normalize task'.

expectations.text: NFRs section visibly accounts for every NFR — either a target or explicit 'N/A — reason'
expectations.evidence: Section 6 walks NFRs in order: Scalability, Reliability, Resilience, Performance, Observability, Security, Cost, Compliance & privacy, Deployment & operations, Disaster recovery, Maintainability, Extensibility. Explicit 'N/A — no end-user PII' for Residency, 'None applicable' for Regulatory, 'No' for Multi-region with rationale.

expectations.text: Contains a 'Risks' / 'Failure modes' section
expectations.evidence: Section '## 7. Risks & failure modes' with table of R-1 through R-9 plus R-EMAIL-1 and R-EMAIL-2 with likelihood, blast radius, mitigation, recovery.

expectations.text: Contains a 'Rollout' section addressing shadow-mode period from the PRD
expectations.evidence: Section '## 8. Rollout & migration' phase 4 'Week 5: Shadow mode for all 12 vendors. Daily review of inventory_audit diffs vs Google Sheet workflow' and phase 5 cutover.

expectations.text: Contains an 'Observability' / 'Operations' section
expectations.evidence: Observability subsection in §6 with metrics, logs, traces, alerts plus dedicated '## 9. Observability & operations' section.

expectations.text: Contains 'Open questions' with at least one entry
expectations.evidence: Section '## 10. Open questions' enumerates OQ-1 through OQ-9.

expectations.text: No fabricated/invented URLs
expectations.evidence: No URLs present; only a placeholder '<link to runbook — to be added>' and an S3 path example. Author noted 'Diagram (describe in prose; no fabricated link)'.

expectations.text: TDD is structured as a scheduled pull-based processing design
expectations.evidence: Celery Beat cron schedules drive REST/SFTP polling with per-vendor cadence_cron, cursors/watermarks, advisory locks for no-overlap — standard scheduled-pull design.

expectations.text: Explicitly acknowledges and handles all 3 ingestion mechanisms (REST, SFTP, email)
expectations.evidence: Section 4.2 contains three subsections: 'REST adapter (8 vendors)', 'SFTP adapter (3 vendors)', and 'Email adapter (Vendor K only)' with mechanism-specific designs.

expectations.text: Proposes a per-vendor connector / adapter pattern with shared core
expectations.evidence: Section 3 calls out 'Three thin ingestion adapters (REST, SFTP, Email)' converging on 'a single normalization-and-apply pipeline'. Extensibility section: 'new adapter implementing the Adapter interface (3 methods: discover_runs(), fetch_to_s3(run), name)'.

expectations.text: Calls out the Vendor F 60 req/hour rate limit and how the design respects it
expectations.evidence: Section 4.2 REST adapter: 'Vendor F is capped at 60 req/hr total — at a 15-min cadence that's 4 runs/hr → 15 pages/run max. The bucket is the enforcement, not the cadence.' Also R-1 in risks and OQ-9.

expectations.text: Discusses per-vendor cursor / watermark / 'since' state
expectations.evidence: Dedicated section '### 4.4 Cursor / watermark and idempotency' with REST cursor, SFTP cursor, Email cursor, first-run lookback. Also vendor_cursor table in data model.

expectations.text: Addresses no-overlap / locking between scheduled runs
expectations.evidence: Section 4.6: 'Postgres advisory lock per (source_type, vendor_id). If lock unavailable, run is skipped'. Also in REST adapter: 'pg_advisory_lock(hashtext(...)) — no two REST runs for the same vendor overlap'.

expectations.text: Defines a freshness SLO and per-vendor stale-data alerting
expectations.evidence: Reliability target: 'Freshness SLO: per-vendor data is at most staleness_threshold_minutes stale, alerted when breached'. Alert P1: 'staleness_seconds > staleness_threshold_minutes * 60 for any vendor for 15+ minutes'.

expectations.text: Addresses storage and rotation of per-vendor credentials
expectations.evidence: Security section: 'Credentials in AWS Secrets Manager, one secret per vendor, IAM-scoped to the worker role' and 'Secrets rotation. Manual on PRD timeline; we expose python manage.py rotate_vendor_secret <vendor_id> to ops'.

expectations.text: Has a concrete plan for the emailed-CSV vendor
expectations.evidence: Section 4.2 'Email adapter (Vendor K only)' details SES inbound rule → S3 → SNS → SQS → handler, Message-ID dedup, attachment regex matching, sender allow-list, staleness alert at 30h.

expectations.text: Designs the shadow-mode rollout phase
expectations.evidence: Per-vendor 'shadow_mode' config flag in §4.1, §4.3 step 8 'In shadow mode, skip the API call and write the proposed change to inventory_audit with applied = false', §8 phase 4 dedicates Week 5 to shadow mode for all 12 vendors with daily diff review.

expectations.text: Designs the audit log of inventory changes
expectations.evidence: Section '### 4.8 Audit log' defines inventory_audit table (vendor_id, run_id, sku, old_qty, new_qty, source_timestamp, applied, applied_at) with index on (sku, applied_at desc) and 18-month retention.
